revision.text:
{{Short description|Remains of Heian period kilns in Suita, Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | native_name = 吉志部瓦窯跡 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = Kisibejinja.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kishibe Jinja, where the Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_caption = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline}} | location = [[Suita, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Heian period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an [[archaeological site]] consisting of the remains of [[Heian period]] [[kiln]]s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[Suita, Osaka|Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[Kishibe Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/kishibegayouatokouen.html Osaka Prefectural Government home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Remains of Heian period kilns in Suita, Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | native_name = 吉志部瓦窯跡 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = Kisibejinja.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kishibe Jinja, where the Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_caption = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline}} | location = [[Suita, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Heian period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an [[archaeological site]] consisting of the remains of [[Heian period]] [[kiln]]s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[Suita, Osaka|Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[Kishibe Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[Kishibe Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an [[archaeological site]] consisting of the remains of [[Heian period]] [[kiln]]s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[Suita, Osaka|Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[Kishibe Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

{{Short description|Remains of Heian period kilns in Suita, Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | native_name = 吉志部瓦窯跡 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = Kisibejinja.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kishibe Jinja, where the Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_caption = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline}} | location = [[Suita, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Heian period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an [[archaeological site]] consisting of the remains of [[Heian period]] [[kiln]]s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[Suita, Osaka|Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[Kishibe Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

{{Short description|Remains of Heian period kilns in Suita, Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | native_name = 吉志部瓦窯跡 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = Kisibejinja.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kishibe Jinja, where the Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_caption = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline}} | location = [[Suita, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Heian period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an [[archaeological site]] consisting of the remains of [[Heian period]] [[kiln]]s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[Suita, Osaka|Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[Kishibe Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[Kishibe Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | native_name = 吉志部瓦窯跡 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = Kisibejinja.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kishibe Jinja, where the Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_caption = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline}} | location = [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] consisting of the remains of [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] [[:en:Kiln|Kiln]]s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[:en:Heian Palace|Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kishibe Station|Kishibe Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/kishibegayouatokouen.html Osaka Prefectural Government home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kishibe Station|Kishibe Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[:en:Heian Palace|Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kishibe Station|Kishibe Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] consisting of the remains of [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] [[:en:Kiln|Kiln]]s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[:en:Heian Palace|Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kishibe Station|Kishibe Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[:en:Heian Palace|Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kishibe Station|Kishibe Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early [[:en:Heian Palace|Heian Palace]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kishibe Station|Kishibe Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|WD=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River}} and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|WD=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kishibe Station|en|Kishibe Station|WD=Q59515|lt=Kishibe Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} consisting of the remains of {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|WD=Q1724341|lt=Kiln}}s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|WD=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River}} and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|WD=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kishibe Station|en|Kishibe Station|WD=Q59515|lt=Kishibe Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/kishibegayouatokouen.html Osaka Prefectural Government home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Remains of Heian period kilns in Suita, Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | native_name = 吉志部瓦窯跡 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = Kisibejinja.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kishibe Jinja, where the Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_caption = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline}} | location = [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} consisting of the remains of {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|WD=Q1724341|lt=Kiln|1=Kiln}}s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|WD=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River|1=Yodo River}} and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|WD=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace|1=Heian Palace}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kishibe Station|en|Kishibe Station|WD=Q59515|lt=Kishibe Station|1=Kishibe Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kishibe-jinja]][[en:Kishibe Tile Kiln Site]][[ja:吉志部神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kishibe-jinja]][[en:Kishibe Tile Kiln Site]][[ja:吉志部神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kishibe Tile Kiln Site]][[ja:吉志部神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kisibejinja.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Suita|qid=Q653510|1=Suita}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.78380555555555|N|135.53105555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}}
{{Short description|Remains of Heian period kilns in Suita, Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | native_name = 吉志部瓦窯跡 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = Kisibejinja.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kishibe Jinja, where the Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_caption = Kishibe tile kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline}} | location = [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|01|N|135|31|51|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kishibe tile kiln ruins'''|吉志部瓦窯跡| Kishibe Kawara-gama ato}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} consisting of the remains of {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|qid=Q1724341|lt=Kiln|1=Kiln}}s located in what is now the Kishibe-kita neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Suita, Osaka|Suita]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1971.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138824|title=吉志部瓦窯跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kishibe tile kiln ruins are located halfway up the southern slope of Shikinzan hills, which extends from the Senri Hills to the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|qid=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River|1=Yodo River}} and is part of the precincts of Kishibe Shrine. The site consists of nine flat kilns and three [[:en:noborigama|climbing kilns]] arranged in two tiers, the upper tier being the climbing kilns and the lower tier being the flat kilns. The flat kilns have a semi-underground structure with a total length of about five meters, and both the combustion and firing sections are well preserved. The climbing kiln has a total length of about six meters and a width of about three meters, and the floor is covered with flat tiles. On the south side of the tile kiln, was the remains of a building with pillars, a well, a rotary table, and a clay mining site. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Green-glazed eaves tiles from the early to late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} were unearthed here, and green-glazed pottery was also found in the climbing kiln, indicating that the kiln was both a pottery and a tile-making kiln. The green-glazed roof tiles were fired in the flat kiln for the first firing and the second firing for the glazing stage in the climbing kiln. Among the excavated tiles, there are tiles that is considered to be identical to the roof tiles used in the construction of the early {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|qid=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace|1=Heian Palace}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is maintained as "Shikinzan Park" and is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kishibe Station|en|Kishibe Station|qid=Q59515|lt=Kishibe Station|1=Kishibe Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Tokaido Main Line|Tokaido Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
